content:
India has a massive insurance gap. Most families are severely underinsured. The question is not just "do you have insurance" but "do you have enough?"

## The Human Life Value (HLV) Method

The most accurate method:

**HLV = Annual Income x Working Years Remaining x 0.65**

**Example:** 35-year-old earning Rs 8 lakh/year, retires at 60:
HLV = Rs 8 lakh x 25 years x 0.65 = **Rs 1.3 crore**

This person needs at least Rs 1.3 crore of life insurance.

## The Simple Rule

**Minimum Life Cover = 10x your annual income**

Rs 5 lakh/year income - Rs 50 lakh minimum cover
Rs 10 lakh/year income - Rs 1 crore minimum cover
Rs 20 lakh/year income - Rs 2 crore minimum cover

Most Indians I meet have Rs 5-10 lakh of LIC coverage against incomes of Rs 8-12 lakh. That is a dangerous gap.

## Adding Up Your Actual Need

1. Replace income for 10-15 years: Rs 6 lakh/year x 12 years = Rs 72 lakh
2. Pay off home loan: Rs 25 lakh
3. Children's education (2 children): Rs 20 lakh
4. Emergency buffer: Rs 5 lakh
**Total: Rs 1.22 crore** - minus your existing Rs 20 lakh LIC cover = **gap of Rs 1.02 crore**

## Cost of Rs 1 Crore Term Insurance

Age 30: approximately Rs 7,000-8,500/year for LIC Jeevan Amar (30-year term)
Age 35: approximately Rs 9,000-11,000/year
Age 40: approximately Rs 13,000-16,000/year

Every year you delay costs money. Calculate your insurance need with our [life insurance calculator](/calculators/life-insurance) or call Ajay Kumar Poddar at 9415313434.
contentHi:
HLV formula: varshik aay x bache hue karya varsh x 0.65. Saral niyam: varshik aay ka 10 guna.

Hamare life insurance calculator se apni zaroorat jaanein. 9415313434 par call karein.
